directives.title: Inspect React Application Performance
steps: Execute the React Doctor diagnostic tool against the target React application.
Analyze the tool's output to identify rendering and performance issues, unnecessary renders, or inefficient update patterns within the React project.
directives.rationale: The phrase 'Run React Doctor to inspect your React app' provides a clear, imperative command. The description of the tool's function (analyzes, identifies issues, inspects component behavior, highlights patterns) allows for the reconstruction of the detailed analysis step.

directives.title: Integrate Persistent Memory into AI Agent Workflow
steps: Integrate the Agent Memory system (an open-source memory layer) into an existing AI agent workflow.
Configure the AI agent to utilize Agent Memory for persistent storage of contextual information, including task history, facts, and interactions.
Implement retrieval mechanisms within the AI agent to access stored contextual information from Agent Memory across sessions during execution to maintain continuity.
directives.target: AI agent system / Agent workflow configuration / Agent codebase
directives.rationale: The command 'Add Agent memory to your agent workflow' is a direct imperative. The detailed description of Agent Memory's capabilities (stores and retrieves context, maintains continuity, preserves task history/facts/interactions, supports persistent retrieval) provides sufficient information to infer the necessary integration, configuration, and usage steps.

directives.title: Configure Cloud-Based Claude Coding Environment
steps: Access the 'Free Cloud Code' open-source repository to retrieve setup instructions, usage examples, and workflow guidance.
Follow the provided setup instructions to configure a cloud-based coding environment for AI-assisted coding, specifically tailored for Claude workflows.
Verify that the configured environment allows for experimentation with Claude workflows without complex onboarding.
directives.target: Cloud coding environment setup (e.g., specific cloud configurations, infrastructure as code files, or provisioning scripts as detailed in the 'Free Cloud Code' repository)
directives.rationale: The transcript explicitly states 'run your own Claude coding setup' and that the 'Free Cloud Code' repository 'provides setup instructions, usage examples, and workflow guidance.' This is a clear directive to execute a configuration process using provided documentation.

directives.title: Deploy application using Doku's Git-based workflow
steps: Configure your local application's Git repository to include a remote pointing to your Doku server instance.
Push the current state of your application's Git repository to the configured Doku remote to initiate deployment.
directives.target: Your Application's Git Repository, Doku Server
directives.rationale: The transcript explicitly states Doku uses 'Git-based deployment workflows' and that 'applications can be pushed directly to servers,' which directly translates to configuring a Git remote and performing a Git push operation for deployment.
